cashFlow.loadError: Failed to load cash flow data
cashFlow.noDataHint: Try selecting a different period or check if you have any transactions
cashFlow.showTrendLineHint: Smoothed average of net cash flow

chart.cumulativeNote: Cumulative total up to this point

trends.noDataHint: No transactions recorded for this period

investmentCalculator.realMultiplierTooltip: How much your total investment grows after accounting for inflation. Unlike nominal returns, this shows real purchasing power. 1.5x means every $1 invested becomes $1.50 in today's money.
investmentCalculator.disclaimer: Based on constant {rate}%/yr return. S&P 500 historical average is ~10%/yr. Does not account for taxes or fees.
investmentCalculator.useAvgNetIncomeTooltip: Your average monthly net income (income minus expenses) over the selected period, based on your transaction history
investmentCalculator.inflationTooltip:
How fast prices rise each year, reducing your money's real value.

~1–2% — very low, stable economy
~2–3% — normal (typical target)
~4–6% — above average
~7%+ — crisis-level inflation

pivotReport.title: Pivot Report
pivotReport.subtitle: Break spending down by any dimension, lay it across time, and compare.

savedViews.dialogDescription: Save the current layout and filters as a reusable view.

states.emptyHint: Try a wider period or different filters.
states.errorHint: Something went wrong. Please try again.

netWorthDrivers.title: Net Worth Drivers
netWorthDrivers.subtitle: How much of your growth you saved, and how much the market returned. Transfers between your own accounts count as neither.

portfolioFilter.scopeHint: Filters investment holdings and growth only. Saved amounts and account cash are not affected.

calcInfo.title: How this is calculated
calcInfo.savedBody: Money you set aside — income minus spending. Moving money between your own accounts, or into an investment portfolio, doesn't count as saving.
calcInfo.grownBody: What the market did to your holdings — price changes, plus dividends, minus fees. Money you put in is never counted as growth.
calcInfo.exampleBody: You earn $10,000, invest it, and it grows to $12,000. That's $10,000 Saved and $2,000 Grown — the $10,000 is counted once, as Saved, and only the $2,000 counts as Grown.
calcInfo.noteBody: Growth is counted as prices move each period, not only when you sell. Selling something that already rose doesn't add to Grown a second time.

chart.title: Saved vs. grown
chart.subtitle: Cumulative totals over the selected period.
chart.allocationContext: {share}% of your net worth sits in investments (was {reference}% in {date})
chart.allocationContextNoReference: {share}% of your net worth sits in investments

degraded.title: Some values are estimated
degraded.unpricedSecurities: No recent price data for {securities}, so their growth is estimated from cost.
degraded.unpricedSecuritiesTruncated: No recent price data for {securities} and others ({count} securities total); their growth is estimated from cost.
degraded.fxFallback: Missing exchange rates for {currencies}; those amounts use a 1:1 rate.

states.noData: Nothing to show for this period
states.noDataHint: Once you have income, expenses, or investments in this range, they'll appear here.
states.noPortfoliosHint: Investment growth appears once you add a portfolio with priced holdings.
states.noPortfoliosPlaceholder: This report splits your net-worth change into what you saved and what your investments grew. Add a portfolio to start tracking growth.

target.title: Growth crossover
target.subtitle: The portfolio size where market growth — not your saving — does most of the work.
target.targetShareLabel: Growth's share of your monthly gain
target.currentShareLine: Today growth covers ~{current}% of your monthly gain — you're aiming for {target}%.
target.progressReached: Target reached — your portfolio is already there.
target.etaHint: If you keep saving and the return holds.
target.noReturn: Pick a positive expected return to see a target value.
target.disclaimer: A straight projection from today's values and your assumptions — not financial advice.

investmentContributions.title: Investment Contributions
investmentContributions.subtitle: How much cash you've moved into your portfolios over time, and where it went.

portfolioFilter.scopeHint: Filters which portfolios count toward contributions.

states.noDataHint: Move money into a portfolio during this period to see it here.
states.noPortfoliosPlaceholder: Create a portfolio and start investing to track your contributions here.
